articles.headline: Eighteen Killed in Meghalaya Illegal Coal Mine Explosion, Raising Concerns Over Worker Safety and Environmental Impact
articles.explainer:
• An explosion in an illegal coal mine in East Jaintia Hills district, Meghalaya, resulted in the death of eighteen workers, highlighting the persistent issue of 'rat-hole' mining.
• The incident brings renewed focus on the dangers faced by miners in unregulated operations and the challenges in enforcing mining bans.
• Environmental activists and local communities have long raised concerns about the ecological damage caused by illegal mining, including water contamination and deforestation.
• The tragedy prompts calls for stricter enforcement, alternative livelihood programs, and improved safety protocols for legal mining operations.
• Investigations are underway to determine the exact cause of the explosion and accountability for the illegal operation.
gsSubTopics: Internal Security challenges
Disaster Management
Environmental Pollution and Degradation
Conservation
Infrastructure: Energy
Inclusive Growth and issues arising from it
prelimsPoints: Location: East Jaintia Hills district, Meghalaya.
Type of mining: Illegal 'rat-hole' coal mining.
Previous NGT ban on rat-hole mining in Meghalaya.
Meghalaya's coal reserves and historical mining practices.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic drivers behind illegal mining in regions like Meghalaya, linking it to livelihood issues and lack of alternative employment.
Analyze the environmental consequences of unregulated mining, including impact on water bodies, forest cover, and biodiversity.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing regulatory frameworks (e.g., NGT bans, Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Act) in preventing illegal mining and ensuring worker safety.
Suggest comprehensive measures to address illegal mining, encompassing stricter enforcement, rehabilitation of miners, sustainable livelihood alternatives, and corporate social responsibility.
Examine the role of state and central governments in monitoring and regulating mining activities, especially in geographically challenging terrains.
articles.imageDescription: Rescue workers near a collapsed mine shaft in a hilly region.
articles.trivia: Rat-hole mining derives its name from the narrow, horizontal tunnels dug into the ground, just wide enough for one person to fit through to extract coal.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns, Focus on Sustainable Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The decision comes as retail inflation shows signs of moderation, though remaining within the RBI's comfort zone of 2-6%.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das emphasized the MPC's commitment to withdrawing accommodation gradually to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• The RBI revised its GDP growth projection for FY27 to 6.8%, citing robust domestic demand and private investment.
• Experts anticipate a potential rate cut later in the year, depending on global economic conditions and monsoon performance.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth
development and employment
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: Current Repo Rate: 6.5%.
MPC's mandate: maintain inflation within 2-6% band.
RBI's GDP growth projection for FY27: 6.8%.
MPC composition: 6 members (3 RBI, 3 government nominees).
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives, especially in the context of global economic uncertainties.
Discuss the tools and instruments available to the RBI under its monetary policy framework and their effectiveness in achieving desired outcomes.
Evaluate the impact of persistent high interest rates on various sectors of the Indian economy, including manufacturing, housing, and exports.
Examine the role of supply-side factors, government fiscal policy, and global commodity prices in influencing domestic inflation trends.
Critically assess the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance and its implications for future liquidity management and interest rate trajectory.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and is entrusted with the task of fixing the benchmark policy interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target.

articles.headline: Government Unveils 'National Digital Health Mission 2.0' to Bolster Healthcare Access and Efficiency
articles.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry launched the 'National Digital Health Mission 2.0' (NDHM 2.0), an upgraded version of the original mission, aiming for universal digital health coverage.
• Key features include a more robust Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) platform, enhanced interoperability of health records, and a focus on teleconsultation services in rural areas.
• NDHM 2.0 seeks to create a seamless online platform for patients to access health services, manage medical records, and connect with healthcare providers nationwide.
• The mission emphasizes data privacy and security, with advanced encryption and consent-based access mechanisms for personal health data.
• Training programs for healthcare professionals and digital literacy initiatives for citizens are integral to ensure widespread adoption and effective utilization of the platform.

articles.headline: ISRO Announces 'Chandrayaan-4' Mission for Lunar Sample Return by 2030
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) officially announced its ambitious 'Chandrayaan-4' mission, targeting a lunar sample return by the end of the decade.
• Building on the successes of Chandrayaan-2 and Chandrayaan-3, this mission aims to collect samples from the Moon's South Pole, an area of significant scientific interest due to potential water ice reserves.
• The mission will involve a sophisticated lander and rover combination, followed by an ascent module to bring lunar soil and rock samples back to Earth for detailed analysis.
• Chandrayaan-4 is a critical step towards India's long-term lunar exploration goals and future manned missions to the Moon.
• International collaboration opportunities for this complex mission are also being explored, leveraging global expertise in space technology.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space

Objective: Lunar sample return.
Target area: Moon's South Pole.

mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of lunar sample return missions for advancing our understanding of the Moon's origin, evolution, and potential resources.
Analyze how India's lunar exploration program (Chandrayaan series) contributes to its scientific prestige and technological capabilities on the global stage.
Evaluate the strategic importance of exploring the Moon's South Pole, particularly in the context of water ice and future lunar habitats.
Examine the challenges involved in a lunar sample return mission, including landing precision, sample collection, and safe return to Earth.
Discuss the potential for international collaboration in such complex space missions and its benefits for scientific advancement and resource sharing.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of a lunar lander and rover collecting samples on the Moon.
articles.trivia: Only the US, Russia (then Soviet Union), and China have successfully returned samples from the Moon to Earth.

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Show Positive Momentum, Key Sectors Identified for Breakthroughs
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the ambitious India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered a crucial phase, with both sides reporting significant progress in several key areas.
• Discussions are focusing on reducing tariffs, improving market access for goods and services, and establishing robust mechanisms for investment protection and dispute resolution.
• Sectors identified for potential breakthroughs include automobiles, textiles, pharmaceuticals, agricultural products, and digital trade.
• The FTA is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses and consumers in both regions.
• Challenges remain in areas like intellectual property rights and sustainability standards, but both parties express commitment to finding common ground.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds 'Right to be Forgotten' in Landmark Data Privacy Ruling
articles.explainer:
• In a significant judgment, the Supreme Court of India affirmed the 'Right to be Forgotten' as an integral aspect of the fundamental Right to Privacy under Article 21 of the Constitution.
• The ruling allows individuals to request the deletion or de-indexing of personal information from public platforms, particularly if it is outdated, irrelevant, or causes undue harm.
• The Court balanced the individual's right to privacy with the public's right to information and freedom of expression, emphasizing a case-by-case assessment.
• This judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for digital platforms, data protection laws, and the handling of personal information in the public domain.
• It reinforces India's commitment to individual data autonomy and aligns with global trends in data protection legislation like GDPR.

Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Separation of powers
mechanisms, institutions and bodies for dispute redressal and their working
e-governance
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court ruling on 'Right to be Forgotten'.
Derived from Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty).

mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of the Right to Privacy in India, from Puttaswamy judgment to the 'Right to be Forgotten', and its implications for individual liberties in the digital age.
Analyze the challenges in implementing the 'Right to be Forgotten', particularly concerning its intersection with freedom of speech and expression, journalistic ethics, and historical records.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in interpreting and expanding fundamental rights to address new challenges posed by technological advancements and data proliferation.
Examine how this ruling will influence the upcoming Data Protection Bill and the responsibilities of data fiduciaries and processors in managing personal information.
Suggest a framework for balancing the 'Right to be Forgotten' with legitimate public interest, national security, and academic research considerations.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: The 'Right to be Forgotten' was first recognized in the European Union through the landmark Google Spain case in 2014, leading to the inclusion in GDPR.

articles.headline: India's Green Hydrogen Mission Accelerates, First Major Hub Project Announced
articles.explainer:
• India's National Green Hydrogen Mission has gained significant momentum with the announcement of the country's first major green hydrogen production and export hub in Gujarat.
• The project aims to leverage India's renewable energy potential to produce green hydrogen and ammonia, positioning India as a global leader in the clean energy transition.
• Financial incentives and policy support under the SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) program are facilitating private sector investment in the nascent industry.
• This initiative is crucial for decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like refineries, fertilizers, and steel, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Skill development and R&D in electrolysis technology are also being prioritized to build an indigenous green hydrogen ecosystem.

articles.headline: Defence Ministry Unveils New Acquisition Policy to Boost Indigenous Manufacturing and Exports
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence launched an updated Defence Acquisition Procedure (DAP) 2026, with a strong emphasis on 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) in defence manufacturing.
• The new policy prioritizes procurement from domestic sources, mandates higher indigenous content in defence platforms, and streamlines the acquisition process.
• It introduces new categories for 'Design & Development' by Indian private industry and encourages joint ventures with foreign OEMs for technology transfer.
• A dedicated section for defence exports has been added, aiming to achieve a target of $5 billion in annual defence exports by 2028.
• The policy also simplifies trial and testing procedures and promotes the use of Artificial Intelligence and emerging technologies in defence production.

articles.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation Progress: Focus on Multidisciplinary Education and Foundational Literacy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a progress report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting significant strides in key areas.
• Emphasis has been placed on transitioning to a multidisciplinary education system, with several universities adopting flexible credit systems and interdisciplinary courses.
• Initiatives like NIPUN Bharat Mission are showing positive outcomes in enhancing foundational literacy and numeracy among young children.
• The report notes increased enrollment in vocational courses and teacher training programs aligned with NEP principles.
• Challenges remain in infrastructure development, digital access in remote areas, and ensuring equitable quality education across all levels.

articles.headline: New Report Warns of Accelerated Himalayan Glacial Melt, Threatening Regional Water Security
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive report by an international consortium of climate scientists highlights an alarming acceleration in the rate of glacial melt across the Himalayas.
• The study predicts severe implications for regional water security, impacting millions who depend on Himalayan rivers for drinking water, agriculture, and hydropower.
• Factors contributing to accelerated melt include rising global temperatures, black carbon deposition, and changes in precipitation patterns.
• The report calls for urgent regional cooperation on climate adaptation strategies and enhanced monitoring of glacial health.
• It also emphasizes the need for sustainable water management policies and investment in climate-resilient infrastructure in downstream nations.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes
prelimsPoints: Himalayan glaciers are melting at an accelerated rate.
Impacts water security for millions in South Asia.

mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted impacts of accelerated Himalayan glacial melt on the ecological balance, water resources, and socio-economic stability of South Asia.
Analyze the anthropogenic and natural factors contributing to glacial retreat in the Himalayas and their implications for long-term water availability.
Evaluate the existing national and regional strategies for climate change adaptation and disaster management in the context of glacial melt and related hazards (e.g., GLOFs).
Examine the geopolitical dimensions of water sharing and transboundary river management in the Himalayan region, considering the challenges posed by reduced glacial meltwater.
Suggest integrated policy measures, including sustainable water management, renewable energy transition, and international collaboration, to address the crisis.
articles.imageDescription: A receding glacier in the Himalayas with visible meltwater streams.
articles.trivia: The Himalayas are often referred to as the 'Third Pole' due to the vast amount of ice and snow they hold, second only to the Arctic and Antarctic.

articles.headline: India Ramps Up Border Infrastructure Along LAC, Focus on Strategic Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• India has significantly accelerated the construction of strategic roads, bridges, and tunnels along the Line of Actual Control (LAC) with China.
• The move aims to enhance military mobility, improve logistics for forward deployment, and provide faster access to remote border areas.
• Projects include all-weather roads, high-altitude bridges, and strategically important tunnels, reducing travel time and operational vulnerabilities.
• This infrastructure push is part of a broader strategy to bolster national security and ensure effective response to any border contingencies.
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is at the forefront of these challenging engineering feats, often operating in harsh terrain and extreme weather conditions.

Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Infrastructure: Roads

Examine the role of border infrastructure in regional development, integrating remote communities, and fostering economic opportunities.

articles.headline: PM-Vishwakarma Scheme Expands to Include New Artisanal Trades, Boosting Rural Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government announced an expansion of the PM-Vishwakarma Scheme, incorporating several new traditional crafts and artisanal trades.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, skill training, modern tools, and market linkages to traditional artisans and craftspeople across various sectors.
• The inclusion of new trades aims to cover a wider segment of the informal economy, preserving traditional skills and promoting indigenous crafts.
• This initiative is central to the government's strategy for empowering the Vishwakarma community and boosting the rural and semi-urban economy.
• Beneficiaries will also receive support for digital transactions and enterprise development, fostering self-employment and small businesses.

articles.headline: Environment Ministry Unveils Stricter Plastic Waste Management Rules, Targets Single-Use Plastics
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change introduced updated and more stringent Plastic Waste Management (Amendment) Rules, 2026.
• The new rules expand the ban on single-use plastic items, introduce extended producer responsibility (EPR) for more categories of plastic products, and mandate higher recycling targets.
• A focus on circular economy principles is evident, promoting the reuse of plastic waste and incentivizing innovation in sustainable alternatives.
• Municipal bodies and urban local bodies are tasked with enhanced collection, segregation, and processing infrastructure for plastic waste.
• Public awareness campaigns and industry collaboration are integral components to ensure effective implementation and behavioral change.

articles.headline: Government Strengthens Cybersecurity Framework for Critical Information Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced new measures to fortify India's cybersecurity framework, particularly for Critical Information Infrastructure (CII).
• The enhanced framework includes mandatory cybersecurity audits, real-time threat intelligence sharing, and stricter data localization norms for entities operating CII.
• Focus areas include energy, finance, telecommunications, transport, and public health sectors, which are vital for national functioning and economy.
• The move comes amidst rising global cyber threats and aims to protect essential services from sophisticated state-sponsored attacks and cyber warfare.
• Public-private partnerships and capacity building for cybersecurity professionals are key components of this upgraded national strategy.

articles.headline: Annual Meeting of Indus Water Treaty's Permanent Commission Concludes, Discussions on Water Flow and Projects
articles.explainer:
• The Permanent Indus Commission, comprising water commissioners from India and Pakistan, concluded its annual meeting in New Delhi.
• Discussions focused on the exchange of hydrological data, flood forecasting information, and technical aspects of various water projects on the Indus basin rivers.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT), a landmark agreement governing the sharing of river waters.
• Minor disputes regarding the design of certain hydropower projects being built by India were also deliberated, with a focus on finding mutually agreeable solutions.
• The meeting underscores the importance of continued dialogue and cooperation between the two nations on transboundary water management.

articles.headline: National Mission for Clean Ganga Reports Significant Progress in River Rejuvenation
articles.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) released its annual report, detailing substantial achievements in pollution abatement and river rejuvenation along the Ganga basin.
• Key milestones include the commissioning of several new Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs), leading to a reduction in untreated sewage discharge into the river.
• Progress was also noted in ghat and crematoria development, afforestation along the riverbanks, and biodiversity conservation efforts.
• Public participation and awareness campaigns, including 'Ganga Praharis' and 'Ganga Mitras', have been instrumental in fostering community ownership.
• Challenges persist in managing industrial pollution, agricultural runoff, and ensuring the long-term sustainability of implemented projects.

articles.headline: India Assumes BIMSTEC Chairmanship, Prioritizes Regional Connectivity and Digital Trade
articles.explainer:
• India has officially taken over the chairmanship of the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC).
• During its tenure, India announced its focus on enhancing regional connectivity, including physical, digital, and energy linkages, to foster greater integration.
• Digital trade facilitation, blue economy initiatives, and strengthening disaster management cooperation are also high on India's agenda.
• BIMSTEC, comprising seven nations from South Asia and Southeast Asia, aims to bridge the Bay of Bengal region through economic and technical cooperation.
• India's leadership is expected to invigorate the grouping, pushing for tangible outcomes in trade, investment, and people-to-people contacts.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Incentive Scheme for Agricultural Diversification Towards High-Value Crops
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare introduced a new incentive scheme aimed at promoting agricultural diversification from traditional crops to high-value alternatives.
• The scheme provides financial support, technical guidance, and market linkages to farmers willing to shift to crops like fruits, vegetables, medicinal plants, and spices.
• The initiative seeks to enhance farmers' income, reduce reliance on water-intensive crops, and meet the growing demand for specialized agricultural products.
• Emphasis is also placed on promoting organic farming practices and value addition through processing and branding of diversified produce.
• Pilot projects are being launched in several states to assess the effectiveness of the incentives and tailor support mechanisms to local agro-climatic conditions.

articles.headline: Scientists Discover New Amphibian Species in Western Ghats, Highlights Biodiversity Hotspot Status
articles.explainer:
• An international team of researchers announced the discovery of a previously unknown species of frog in the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats of India.
• The new amphibian, named 'Nyctibatrachus sahyadriensis' (Sahyadri Night Frog), was found in a remote forest patch, underscoring the region's unique ecological value.
• This discovery highlights the urgent need for continued exploration and robust conservation efforts in the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a global biodiversity hotspot.
• The frog's unique characteristics and habitat preferences provide valuable insights into amphibian evolution and ecological indicators.
• Scientists emphasize that many more species likely await discovery in the largely unexplored pockets of the Ghats, facing threats from habitat loss and climate change.

Biodiversity

articles.headline: Archaeologists Unearth New Insights from Ancient Harappan Settlement in Haryana
articles.explainer:
• Ongoing excavations at an ancient Harappan settlement in Rakhigarhi, Haryana, have yielded significant new archaeological findings.
• Researchers have uncovered evidence of advanced urban planning, elaborate drainage systems, and intricate craft workshops, indicating a flourishing civilization.
• The discoveries include pottery, seals, tools, and skeletal remains, providing crucial data on the lifestyle, trade networks, and social structures of the Harappans.
• Rakhigarhi is one of the largest Harappan sites, and these findings further solidify its importance in understanding the Indus Valley Civilization.
• The excavation work employs modern archaeological techniques, including remote sensing and DNA analysis, to unlock the mysteries of this ancient past.

articles.trivia: Rakhigarhi is believed to be older than Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa, making it a potentially crucial site for understanding the origins of the Indus Valley Civilization.

articles.headline: India Initiates Stakeholder Consultations for a Comprehensive AI Regulation Framework
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has initiated extensive stakeholder consultations to develop a comprehensive regulatory framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) in India.
• The discussions aim to balance innovation and responsible AI development with ethical considerations, data privacy, and potential societal impacts.
• Key areas of focus include AI governance, data biases, accountability for AI decisions, and ensuring the safe and secure deployment of AI systems.
• India seeks to establish itself as a leader in AI innovation while also setting global standards for ethical AI use.
• Input is being sought from industry experts, academia, civil society organizations, and international bodies to craft a nuanced and forward-looking policy.

articles.headline: Project Cheetah Shows Promising Results, New Cub Births Boost Conservation Efforts
articles.explainer:
• Project Cheetah, India's ambitious reintroduction program, has reported encouraging progress with the successful birth of new cheetah cubs in Kuno National Park.
• These births mark a significant milestone in the effort to establish a viable cheetah population in India, decades after their extinction in the country.
• The project involves careful monitoring of the introduced cheetahs, habitat management, and community engagement to ensure their long-term survival.
• Challenges include adaptation to the new environment, inter-species competition, and managing human-wildlife interface issues.
• The success of Project Cheetah is seen as a crucial step in global conservation efforts for this vulnerable species and a boost to India's biodiversity restoration initiatives.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent coal mine explosion in Meghalaya, consider the following statements:
1. The incident occurred in West Jaintia Hills district.
2. The mining practice involved is commonly known as 'rat-hole mining'.
3. The National Green Tribunal (NGT) has previously banned this form of mining in Meghalaya.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The incident occurred in East Jaintia Hills district, not West Jaintia Hills. Statement 2 is correct: The illegal mining practice is indeed referred to as 'rat-hole mining'. Statement 3 is correct: The National Green Tribunal (NGT) had imposed a ban on rat-hole mining in Meghalaya in 2014, which has often been violated.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is primarily responsible for fixing the benchmark policy interest rate (repo rate) in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: While the RBI implements monetary policy, the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is the statutory body entrusted with the responsibility of fixing the benchmark policy interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target within the specified tolerance band. The MPC consists of six members, with three from the RBI and three appointed by the government.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'National Digital Health Mission 2.0':
1. It aims for universal digital health coverage across India.
2. The mission's core component is the 'National Health Stack'.
3. It emphasizes data privacy and security through consent-based access mechanisms.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: NDHM 2.0 aims for universal digital health coverage. Statement 2 is incorrect: The mission's core component is the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) platform, which builds upon the concept of a 'National Health Stack'. Statement 3 is correct: Data privacy and security, with consent-based access, are emphasized in the mission.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Right to be Forgotten' has recently been affirmed by the Supreme Court of India as an integral aspect of which fundamental right?
options: (b) Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression (Article 19)
(c) Right to Life and Personal Liberty (Article 21)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Right to Life and Personal Liberty (Article 21)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court, building on its Puttaswamy judgment, has affirmed the 'Right to be Forgotten' as an intrinsic part of the fundamental Right to Privacy, which itself is an integral part of the Right to Life and Personal Liberty under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the National Green Hydrogen Mission and the SIGHT program is/are correct?
1. The mission aims to make India a net importer of green hydrogen.
2. The SIGHT program provides financial incentives for green hydrogen production and manufacturing of electrolysers.
3. Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water using fossil fuels.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, making it a net exporter. Statement 2 is correct: The SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) program offers financial incentives for promoting green hydrogen production and manufacturing of electrolysers. Statement 3 is incorrect: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water using renewable electricity (electrolysis), not fossil fuels.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the multi-dimensional challenges posed by illegal mining, particularly 'rat-hole mining', in ecologically sensitive regions like Meghalaya. GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds 'Right to be Forgotten' in Landmark Data Privacy Ruling
GS2.explainer:
• In a significant judgment, the Supreme Court of India affirmed the 'Right to be Forgotten' as an integral aspect of the fundamental Right to Privacy under Article 21 of the Constitution.
• The ruling allows individuals to request the deletion or de-indexing of personal information from public platforms, particularly if it is outdated, irrelevant, or causes undue harm.
• The Court balanced the individual's right to privacy with the public's right to information and freedom of expression, emphasizing a case-by-case assessment.
• This judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for digital platforms, data protection laws, and the handling of personal information in the public domain.
• It reinforces India's commitment to individual data autonomy and aligns with global trends in data protection legislation like GDPR.

GS2.headline: Annual Meeting of Indus Water Treaty's Permanent Commission Concludes, Discussions on Water Flow and Projects
GS2.explainer:
• The Permanent Indus Commission, comprising water commissioners from India and Pakistan, concluded its annual meeting in New Delhi.
• Discussions focused on the exchange of hydrological data, flood forecasting information, and technical aspects of various water projects on the Indus basin rivers.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT), a landmark agreement governing the sharing of river waters.
• Minor disputes regarding the design of certain hydropower projects being built by India were also deliberated, with a focus on finding mutually agreeable solutions.
• The meeting underscores the importance of continued dialogue and cooperation between the two nations on transboundary water management.

GS2.headline: India Assumes BIMSTEC Chairmanship, Prioritizes Regional Connectivity and Digital Trade
GS2.explainer:
• India has officially taken over the chairmanship of the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC).
• During its tenure, India announced its focus on enhancing regional connectivity, including physical, digital, and energy linkages, to foster greater integration.
• Digital trade facilitation, blue economy initiatives, and strengthening disaster management cooperation are also high on India's agenda.
• BIMSTEC, comprising seven nations from South Asia and Southeast Asia, aims to bridge the Bay of Bengal region through economic and technical cooperation.
• India's leadership is expected to invigorate the grouping, pushing for tangible outcomes in trade, investment, and people-to-people contacts.

GS3.headline: Eighteen Killed in Meghalaya Illegal Coal Mine Explosion, Raising Concerns Over Worker Safety and Environmental Impact
GS3.explainer:
• An explosion in an illegal coal mine in East Jaintia Hills district, Meghalaya, resulted in the death of eighteen workers, highlighting the persistent issue of 'rat-hole' mining.
• The incident brings renewed focus on the dangers faced by miners in unregulated operations and the challenges in enforcing mining bans.
• Environmental activists and local communities have long raised concerns about the ecological damage caused by illegal mining, including water contamination and deforestation.
• The tragedy prompts calls for stricter enforcement, alternative livelihood programs, and improved safety protocols for legal mining operations.
• Investigations are underway to determine the exact cause of the explosion and accountability for the illegal operation.

Disaster Management

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns, Focus on Sustainable Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The decision comes as retail inflation shows signs of moderation, though remaining within the RBI's comfort zone of 2-6%.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das emphasized the MPC's commitment to withdrawing accommodation gradually to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• The RBI revised its GDP growth projection for FY27 to 6.8%, citing robust domestic demand and private investment.
• Experts anticipate a potential rate cut later in the year, depending on global economic conditions and monsoon performance.

GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and is entrusted with the task of fixing the benchmark policy interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target.

GS3.headline: ISRO Announces 'Chandrayaan-4' Mission for Lunar Sample Return by 2030
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) officially announced its ambitious 'Chandrayaan-4' mission, targeting a lunar sample return by the end of the decade.
• Building on the successes of Chandrayaan-2 and Chandrayaan-3, this mission aims to collect samples from the Moon's South Pole, an area of significant scientific interest due to potential water ice reserves.
• The mission will involve a sophisticated lander and rover combination, followed by an ascent module to bring lunar soil and rock samples back to Earth for detailed analysis.
• Chandrayaan-4 is a critical step towards India's long-term lunar exploration goals and future manned missions to the Moon.
• International collaboration opportunities for this complex mission are also being explored, leveraging global expertise in space technology.

GS3.headline: India's Green Hydrogen Mission Accelerates, First Major Hub Project Announced
GS3.explainer:
• India's National Green Hydrogen Mission has gained significant momentum with the announcement of the country's first major green hydrogen production and export hub in Gujarat.
• The project aims to leverage India's renewable energy potential to produce green hydrogen and ammonia, positioning India as a global leader in the clean energy transition.
• Financial incentives and policy support under the SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) program are facilitating private sector investment in the nascent industry.
• This initiative is crucial for decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like refineries, fertilizers, and steel, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Skill development and R&D in electrolysis technology are also being prioritized to build an indigenous green hydrogen ecosystem.

GS3.headline: Defence Ministry Unveils New Acquisition Policy to Boost Indigenous Manufacturing and Exports
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence launched an updated Defence Acquisition Procedure (DAP) 2026, with a strong emphasis on 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) in defence manufacturing.
• The new policy prioritizes procurement from domestic sources, mandates higher indigenous content in defence platforms, and streamlines the acquisition process.
• It introduces new categories for 'Design & Development' by Indian private industry and encourages joint ventures with foreign OEMs for technology transfer.
• A dedicated section for defence exports has been added, aiming to achieve a target of $5 billion in annual defence exports by 2028.
• The policy also simplifies trial and testing procedures and promotes the use of Artificial Intelligence and emerging technologies in defence production.

GS3.headline: New Report Warns of Accelerated Himalayan Glacial Melt, Threatening Regional Water Security
GS3.explainer:
• A comprehensive report by an international consortium of climate scientists highlights an alarming acceleration in the rate of glacial melt across the Himalayas.
• The study predicts severe implications for regional water security, impacting millions who depend on Himalayan rivers for drinking water, agriculture, and hydropower.
• Factors contributing to accelerated melt include rising global temperatures, black carbon deposition, and changes in precipitation patterns.
• The report calls for urgent regional cooperation on climate adaptation strategies and enhanced monitoring of glacial health.
• It also emphasizes the need for sustainable water management policies and investment in climate-resilient infrastructure in downstream nations.

GS3.headline: India Ramps Up Border Infrastructure Along LAC, Focus on Strategic Connectivity
GS3.explainer:
• India has significantly accelerated the construction of strategic roads, bridges, and tunnels along the Line of Actual Control (LAC) with China.
• The move aims to enhance military mobility, improve logistics for forward deployment, and provide faster access to remote border areas.
• Projects include all-weather roads, high-altitude bridges, and strategically important tunnels, reducing travel time and operational vulnerabilities.
• This infrastructure push is part of a broader strategy to bolster national security and ensure effective response to any border contingencies.
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is at the forefront of these challenging engineering feats, often operating in harsh terrain and extreme weather conditions.

GS3.headline: Environment Ministry Unveils Stricter Plastic Waste Management Rules, Targets Single-Use Plastics
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change introduced updated and more stringent Plastic Waste Management (Amendment) Rules, 2026.
• The new rules expand the ban on single-use plastic items, introduce extended producer responsibility (EPR) for more categories of plastic products, and mandate higher recycling targets.
• A focus on circular economy principles is evident, promoting the reuse of plastic waste and incentivizing innovation in sustainable alternatives.
• Municipal bodies and urban local bodies are tasked with enhanced collection, segregation, and processing infrastructure for plastic waste.
• Public awareness campaigns and industry collaboration are integral components to ensure effective implementation and behavioral change.

GS3.headline: Government Strengthens Cybersecurity Framework for Critical Information Infrastructure
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced new measures to fortify India's cybersecurity framework, particularly for Critical Information Infrastructure (CII).
• The enhanced framework includes mandatory cybersecurity audits, real-time threat intelligence sharing, and stricter data localization norms for entities operating CII.
• Focus areas include energy, finance, telecommunications, transport, and public health sectors, which are vital for national functioning and economy.
• The move comes amidst rising global cyber threats and aims to protect essential services from sophisticated state-sponsored attacks and cyber warfare.
• Public-private partnerships and capacity building for cybersecurity professionals are key components of this upgraded national strategy.

GS3.headline: National Mission for Clean Ganga Reports Significant Progress in River Rejuvenation
GS3.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) released its annual report, detailing substantial achievements in pollution abatement and river rejuvenation along the Ganga basin.
• Key milestones include the commissioning of several new Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs), leading to a reduction in untreated sewage discharge into the river.
• Progress was also noted in ghat and crematoria development, afforestation along the riverbanks, and biodiversity conservation efforts.
• Public participation and awareness campaigns, including 'Ganga Praharis' and 'Ganga Mitras', have been instrumental in fostering community ownership.
• Challenges persist in managing industrial pollution, agricultural runoff, and ensuring the long-term sustainability of implemented projects.

GS3.headline: Government Launches New Incentive Scheme for Agricultural Diversification Towards High-Value Crops
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare introduced a new incentive scheme aimed at promoting agricultural diversification from traditional crops to high-value alternatives.
• The scheme provides financial support, technical guidance, and market linkages to farmers willing to shift to crops like fruits, vegetables, medicinal plants, and spices.
• The initiative seeks to enhance farmers' income, reduce reliance on water-intensive crops, and meet the growing demand for specialized agricultural products.
• Emphasis is also placed on promoting organic farming practices and value addition through processing and branding of diversified produce.
• Pilot projects are being launched in several states to assess the effectiveness of the incentives and tailor support mechanisms to local agro-climatic conditions.

GS3.headline: Scientists Discover New Amphibian Species in Western Ghats, Highlights Biodiversity Hotspot Status
GS3.explainer:
• An international team of researchers announced the discovery of a previously unknown species of frog in the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats of India.
• The new amphibian, named 'Nyctibatrachus sahyadriensis' (Sahyadri Night Frog), was found in a remote forest patch, underscoring the region's unique ecological value.
• This discovery highlights the urgent need for continued exploration and robust conservation efforts in the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a global biodiversity hotspot.
• The frog's unique characteristics and habitat preferences provide valuable insights into amphibian evolution and ecological indicators.
• Scientists emphasize that many more species likely await discovery in the largely unexplored pockets of the Ghats, facing threats from habitat loss and climate change.

GS3.headline: India Initiates Stakeholder Consultations for a Comprehensive AI Regulation Framework
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has initiated extensive stakeholder consultations to develop a comprehensive regulatory framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) in India.
• The discussions aim to balance innovation and responsible AI development with ethical considerations, data privacy, and potential societal impacts.
• Key areas of focus include AI governance, data biases, accountability for AI decisions, and ensuring the safe and secure deployment of AI systems.
• India seeks to establish itself as a leader in AI innovation while also setting global standards for ethical AI use.
• Input is being sought from industry experts, academia, civil society organizations, and international bodies to craft a nuanced and forward-looking policy.

GS3.headline: Project Cheetah Shows Promising Results, New Cub Births Boost Conservation Efforts
GS3.explainer:
• Project Cheetah, India's ambitious reintroduction program, has reported encouraging progress with the successful birth of new cheetah cubs in Kuno National Park.
• These births mark a significant milestone in the effort to establish a viable cheetah population in India, decades after their extinction in the country.
• The project involves careful monitoring of the introduced cheetahs, habitat management, and community engagement to ensure their long-term survival.
• Challenges include adaptation to the new environment, inter-species competition, and managing human-wildlife interface issues.
• The success of Project Cheetah is seen as a crucial step in global conservation efforts for this vulnerable species and a boost to India's biodiversity restoration initiatives.

prelimsPoints: Project Cheetah reintroduces cheetahs to India.
Successful cub births reported in Kuno National Park, Madhya Pradesh.
Cheetahs were declared extinct in India in 1952.
Aims to establish a viable cheetah population.
Part of global conservation efforts for cheetahs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and conservation significance of Project Cheetah in India, considering the historical context of cheetah extinction and reintroduction challenges.
Analyze the scientific and logistical complexities involved in a large-scale wildlife reintroduction program, including habitat suitability, prey base, and genetic diversity.
Evaluate the socio-economic impacts of Project Cheetah on local communities, including potential benefits from ecotourism and challenges related to human-wildlife coexistence.
Examine the role of Kuno National Park as a suitable habitat for cheetahs and the measures taken for its management and protection.
Suggest strategies for ensuring the long-term success and sustainability of Project Cheetah, including monitoring, research, community engagement, and adaptive management.
GS3.imageDescription: A cheetah mother with her cubs in the grasslands of Kuno National Park.
GS3.trivia: The cheetah is the only large carnivore to have been completely wiped out from India, primarily due to hunting and habitat loss.
